channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Negotiate remote pay from low-cost countries
item.description: Raise your remote rate 21% with a 37-line Python script that converts your local cost into USD and builds a finance-ready PDF—no Delaware C-corp required.

item.title: Break RAG at 10k requests/day
item.description: Cut RAG latency from 4.2s to 85ms and AWS costs 78% with Qdrant 1.8.3, quantized embeddings, and Spot Instances — the exact stack and numbers most tutorials skip

item.title: Your portfolio doesn't need a website
item.description: Portfolio sites waste time for remote engineers. GitHub, blog posts, and production demos land interviews faster — here’s the 3-2-1 proof with 2026 data.

item.title: Senior devs flee when systems rot
item.description: Senior devs quit big tech for 3 invisible reasons — here’s what to measure next. Cache hit ratio, pager frequency, and connection leaks reveal the real technical debt.

item.title: Pool size: too high, not low, kills speed
item.description: In my experience, it works fine for small apps or internal tools, but as soon as you hit 10,000–20,000 requests per second, the math breaks down.

item.title: AI skills worth $120k in 2026
item.description: Python vs SQL AI stacks in 2026: latency, cost, and salary impact — backed by 47k survey responses and real infra bills. See the 89 ms vs 1,200 ms gap.

item.title: 2026: Which AI skill pays more?
item.description: TensorFlow or PyTorch skills pay 8–12% more in 2026 — the benchmark that separates regulated fintech roles from seed-stage healthtechs

item.title: Remote devs in Africa: sell impact, not code
item.description: Portfolio wins: show impact, not code dumps — reduce latency 60%, cut costs 42%, and land remote jobs from Africa with measurable outcomes.

item.title: Pay yourself 3x with Remote Salary Math
item.description: Pay yourself 3x more by converting local costs to USD with real data, clauses, and tools — avoid the 8–12% loss most remote workers miss in 2026.

item.title: Size your pool: measure, don’t guess
item.description: Cut connection pool waste: measure database capacity first, not CPU cores. Benchmarks from PostgreSQL 16 on RDS show 6x latency improvement when pool size matches database limits.

item.title: Senior devs quit big tech for this
item.description: Senior engineers leave big tech even when pay is high — hidden friction in review cycles, on-call rotations, and deployment pipelines burns them out. Here are the real reasons with actionable fixes.

item.title: Underrated PostgreSQL limits in RAG pipelines
item.description: Reduce RAG latency from 800 ms to 60 ms at 1,000 QPS by replacing PostgreSQL with Qdrant and adding Redis caching. Real AWS cost drop from $2.40 to $0.72 per hour and embedding latency cut 2.6x with int8 quantisation.

item.title: Fine-tuning loses to prompt design 90% of the time
item.description: Fine-tuning loses to prompt design 90% of the time — latency, cost, and iteration speed wins 9 out of 10 times. Real systems data with numbers, code, and mistakes.

item.title: Mis-size DB pool in 2026?
item.description: Cut API latency 86% with PostgreSQL pooling — the max pool size formula most teams still copy from 2010 guides is wrong in 2026. Real benchmarks, async tuning, and the one setting you’re probably missing.

item.title: AI salaries 2026: skills that pay vs hype
item.description: Cut 2026 AI salary volatility: only two skills move the needle — RAG evaluation and privacy-preserving ML. Benchmarks, compliance risks, and the exact hiring data you need to pick the right one.

item.title: RAG in prod: what breaks at scale
item.description: Cut RAG pipeline latency 87% and AWS bill 48% by decoupling nightly embeddings and caching retrievals — connection pool traps, Qdrant memory tricks, and Parquet snapshots we learned the hard way.

item.title: Senior devs flee big tech (not just for cash)
item.description: Senior engineers quit big tech not for money but for autonomy and impact — backed by 2026 data and real stories from 47 engineers. See the hidden reasons and how to measure your own workplace freedom.

item.title: Show Africa devs: ship code, not GitHub stars
item.description: Show production-grade code, not GitHub stars. Build 4 minimal services with pytest 7.4, k6, and Terraform 1.6 to land remote jobs in 2026.

item.title: Pay raise email that works in 2026
item.description: Negotiate remote salaries from lower-cost countries with a proven 2026 system: anchor to US bands, convert to PPP, and close 35%+ increases in 14 days on average.

item.title: Redis vs Memcached: choose the right cache
item.description: Cut API response time 70% with the right cache — Redis vs Memcached 2026 benchmarks, real p99 latency, and the one config change that prevents 70% of outages.
